Zero contact roles carry Champion designation—only Anees Azeez (Procurement Specialist, Evaluator), a gatekeeper not an advocate. day_summary.overall: Turnwell saw a major upward reset today: deal size tripled from $100K to $300K and the close date pulled forward nearly nine months to October 2026, signaling a real scope expansion or a newly qualified, more urgent opportunity. But the health signals tell a cautionary story underneath the good news — the account is under-mapped (no confirmed decision maker, thin org chart, low inbound engagement, 10+ days of silence), so the bigger number now needs real relationship-building to back it up. Net: we've raised our commitment on paper, but we're the ones who need to re-engage the buyer, not the other way around.

cro_panel.intro: Turnwell_S2C_2026 is a $300K S2C opportunity moving through a formal RFQ/tender (RFQ 5000001579) run entirely through a local partner, International Development Company LLC.; Zycus completed the internal LOI/pre-qualification package and handed it to the partner on 1 Jul 2026, but the partner's promised confirmation of official submission and gate-1 outcome (due 8 Jul 2026) has not yet come through as of 9 Jul 2026.; No economic buyer, champion, or competitor has been identified, and the only known contact is a single procurement evaluator, Anees Azeez

value.detail: Close date 24 October 2026 (115 days out) is optimistic for a $300k, 4-product S2C transformation that is still at LOI gate (Formal Evaluation stage) with no EB mapped, no champion, no quantified business case, and no downstream milestones scheduled. Even if Zycus passes the LOI (update expected 8 July), the remaining sequence—RFP response, shortlisting, vendor selection, commercials, ROI validation, EB presentation, legal/contract—cannot realistically complete by late October without a major acceleration. The deal will require at least one stage slip (likely to Q1 2027) unless the buyer compresses the timeline or a forcing function (incumbent contract expiration, fiscal-year budget deadline) emerges. No such forcing function is documented (Current_Contract_Expiration__c null).
